source_record.title_en: Design and modeling of a dual-archimedes screw deep drilling system for lunar subsurface exploration
source_record.title_zh: 月球亚表面探测用双阿基米德螺旋钻进系统设计与建模
source_record.abstract: Studying profound samples from celestial entities like the Moon and Mars offers a gateway to comprehending the chronicles of our solar system. These samples encapsulate the authentic geological insights from its inception. Nonetheless, procuring these invaluable scientific specimens demands attaining the desired depths and surmounting the formidable hurdle of penetrating through the extensive regolith layers. This study presents a novel concept for an autonomous self-burrowing drilling system based on the Archimedes screw principle. The drilling robot is designed in a mole-type configuration and employs an innovative dual-Archimedes screw (or dual-screw) regolith conveying method. A prototype of the robot is developed, designed, and manufactured. To assess the robotic system's performance, a model is formulated to represent the drilling load within the regolith and is derived accordingly. A ground test platform is established to validate both the analytical model and the mechanical capabilities of the robotic system, using in-house lunar regolith simulants CSU-LRS-1. The robot undergoes a parametric analysis under specific manipulation modes. The experimental results unveil the intricate nature of the drilling load, influenced by the varying kinematic states of the conveyed regolith. Additionally, the mechanical specific energy approach is introduced to enhance the understanding of how controlled dual-screw rotation affects the robot's drilling resistance and operational efficiency. The proposed drilling load model concurs with the observed trend of drilling load changes as depth increases. The research, covering theory and experimentation, is dedicated to overcoming critical obstacles in the envisioned lunar deep drilling system. This will propel the advancement of multi-depth sampling technology and deep directional drilling or locomotion capabilities. © 2024 IAA

screening_info.reason: 标题显示研究了基于阿基米德螺旋原理的自主钻进系统，摘要提到了钻进机器人的设计和模型，以及在模拟月壤中的实验验证。

content_summary.one_sentence_summary: 本研究提出了一种基于双阿基米德螺旋原理的月球亚表面探测钻进系统，并通过实验验证了其性能。
content_summary.core_summary: 该研究设计了一种新型的自主钻进机器人，采用双阿基米德螺旋原理来实现月壤的钻进和输送。通过建立模型和地面测试平台，研究了钻进负载与月壤运动状态的关系，并引入机械比能方法来分析钻进阻力和效率。
content_summary.research_focus: 月球亚表面探测钻进系统设计与实验验证
content_summary.value_for_topic: 提供了一种针对月球亚表面钻进的创新设计方案，对地下钻进机器人的推进与破土机制研究具有重要参考价值。
content_summary.limitations: 摘要中未提及具体的实验结果数据，对钻进系统在不同地质条件下的适应性也未作讨论。

classification.core_problem: 如何设计一种有效的月球亚表面钻进系统以克服月壤层的障碍

source_record.title_en: Multiscale analysis of rotational penetration in shallow dry sand and implications for self-burrowing robot design
source_record.title_zh: 干沙中旋转穿透的多尺度分析及其对自埋机器人设计的启示
source_record.abstract: In nature, seeds of some flowering plants such as Erodium and Pelargonium can bury themselves into the ground effectively for germination. Jung et al. (Phys Fluids 29:041702. https://doi.org/10.1063/1.4979998, 2017) hypothesized that rotation induced by the hygroscopic coiling and uncoiling movement of the awn reduces the penetration resistance. Rotational penetration was also studied in geotechnical engineering, as it is relevant to the rotary installation of piles. However, there are limited fundamental explanations of the effect of rotation on the reduction of penetration resistance. In this study, shallow rotational penetration in dry sand is studied using the discrete element method (DEM); the directly available particle-scale data and the derived meso-scale data were analyzed to reveal the underlying mechanism of the rotational effect on penetration. A series of rotational penetration tests with different rotational speeds were conducted. It was confirmed that the penetration resistance at the cone decreases with rotational speed. Analysis of the particle–cone contact data shows that rotation does not only result in the inclination of the contact forces, but also significantly reduces their magnitude and the overall contact number. The force chain network, displacement fields and particle trajectories visualize the rotational effects at the particle-scale; and the evolution of the principal stresses of the soil provides a meso-scale explanation. The new multi-scale data tested the “force chain breakage” hypothesis and challenged the assumptions previously used in developing analytical models. Insights were also provided to power consumption and implications on the design of a self-burrowing robot, which could take advantage of the rotational effect on penetration. © 2022, The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag GmbH Germany, part of Springer Nature.

screening_info.reason: 标题和摘要均提到了旋转穿透在干沙中的研究，与自埋机器人设计相关，符合研究主题。

content_summary.one_sentence_summary: 研究通过离散元素法分析干沙中旋转穿透的多尺度效应，为自埋机器人设计提供洞见。
content_summary.core_summary: 本研究利用离散元素法(DEM)探究干沙中旋转穿透现象，分析不同旋转速度下的穿透阻力变化，揭示旋转对减少穿透阻力的影响机制。实验结果表明，旋转不仅使接触力倾斜，还显著降低了接触力的大小和接触数量，为自埋机器人设计提供新思路。
content_summary.research_focus: 旋转穿透效应对自埋机器人设计的影响
content_summary.value_for_topic: 提供了旋转穿透在减少土壤穿透阻力中的作用机制，对设计地下钻进机器人具有直接参考价值。
content_summary.limitations: 研究主要在干沙环境中进行，其结论可能不完全适用于其他类型的土壤环境。

classification.core_problem: 旋转穿透如何减少土壤穿透阻力

source_record.title_en: A bio-inspired helically driven self-burrowing robot
source_record.title_zh: 仿生螺旋驱动自埋机器人
source_record.abstract: Autonomous subsurface applications (e.g., construction, exploration, and environmental monitoring) have created a need for burrowing mechanisms and robots. This study presents a bio-inspired burrowing robot and explores its burrowing behavior (in terms of speed, acceleration, energetics, and cost of transport) in glass beads used as a sand analog. The robot has two main segments: an anchoring central body and a screw driving body. Two different screw designs (a one- and four-bladed screw) and three different anchoring fins (a dichotomous, tubercled, and control fin) were tested. It was observed that while the four-bladed screw provides a higher translational velocity, it came at the expense of higher motor torque and power, making the cost of transport high. It was also discovered that operating the one-bladed screw at a lower rotational speed provided a lower cost of transport both when burrowing in and out. In addition, the tubercled fin design showed promising results for decreasing vertical drag and, thus, increasing the translational velocity as the robot burrows into the granular media. The knowledge gained through this series of experiments will assist in the optimization of effective burrowing robots for geotechnical and geoenvironmental applications such as site investigation and environmental monitoring. © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag GmbH Germany, part of Springer Nature 2023.

screening_info.reason: 标题和摘要均提到了仿生螺旋驱动的自埋机器人，与地下钻进机器人推进与破土机制相关。

content_summary.one_sentence_summary: 研究了一种仿生螺旋驱动自埋机器人在沙土模拟环境中的掘进行为。
content_summary.core_summary: 本研究介绍了一种仿生螺旋驱动自埋机器人，并在玻璃珠模拟沙土环境中测试了其掘进行为，包括速度、加速度、能耗和运输成本。研究发现四叶螺旋设计虽然提高了掘进速度，但增加了电机扭矩和功率消耗，导致运输成本较高。而单叶螺旋在较低转速下运行时，无论是掘进还是退出，都能降低运输成本。此外，结节状鳍设计在减少垂直阻力、提高掘进速度方面显示出潜力。
content_summary.research_focus: 仿生自埋机器人设计与掘进效率
content_summary.value_for_topic: 提供了地下钻进机器人推进与破土机制研究中关于仿生设计和掘进效率优化的实验数据和分析。
content_summary.limitations: 研究局限在于仅在模拟沙土环境中进行了测试，实际应用场景的适应性尚未验证。

classification.core_problem: 如何提高地下钻进机器人的掘进效率和降低能耗。
